notes.title: Welcome to Scratchpad
notes.body:
# Welcome to Scratchpad

This is a **privacy-first, local-only** notes app. Everything you write lives in *this browser* — no account, no sync, no servers. To prove it, open your browser's network tab: after the page loads, Scratchpad makes **zero** network calls.

This sample backup is a guided tour. Poke at everything, then clear it and make the app yours.

## Try these first

- [x] Import this backup (you just did)
- [ ] Click a [[Markdown Guide|wikilink]] to jump between notes
- [ ] Open [[Project: Trailhead]] and check off a task
- [ ] Pin or unpin a note to reorder the sidebar
- [ ] Open the command palette with `Cmd/Ctrl + K`
- [ ] Export your own backup from the ⋯ menu

## What's in this backup

| Note | Shows off |
| --- | --- |
| [[Markdown Guide]] | Every formatting trick |
| [[Project: Trailhead]] | Task lists, tables, version history |
| [[Kickoff Meeting]] | Meeting notes + action items |
| [[Reading List]] | Checklists with links |
| [[Weeknight Pasta]] | A note you'd actually keep |
| [[Ideas Parking Lot]] | Backlinks and phantom links |

> Notes that link to each other show up under **Linked from** at the bottom of a note. That's how Scratchpad quietly builds a web of your thinking.

Learn more at [notes.vinny.dev](https://notes.vinny.dev).

notes.title: Markdown Guide
notes.body:
# Markdown Guide

Scratchpad renders standard (GitHub-flavored) Markdown, and the output is sanitized — so pasting from anywhere is safe.

## Text styling

Write **bold**, *italic*, ***both***, ~~strikethrough~~, and `inline code`. Break sections with a horizontal rule:

---

## Lists

Nested unordered lists:

- Groceries
  - Coffee
  - Oat milk
- Errands
  - Post office

Ordered lists:

1. First
2. Second
3. Third

Task lists are **interactive** — toggle the boxes right in the rendered note:

- [x] Ship the thing
- [x] Tell everyone
- [ ] Rest

## Quotes

> "The palest ink is better than the best memory."

## Code

Inline `const x = 42;`, or fenced blocks with a language hint:

```js
function greet(name) {
  return `Hello, ${name}!`;
}
```

```python
def total(items):
    return sum(i.price for i in items)
```

## Tables

| Feature | Supported | Notes |
| --- | :---: | --- |
| Headings | yes | `#` through `######` |
| Tables | yes | like this one |
| Task lists | yes | click to toggle |
| Wikilinks | yes | `[[Note Title]]` |

## Links

- External links open in a new tab: [Inkwell design system](https://github.com/vscarpenter/inkwell)
- Internal links use double brackets: [[Welcome to Scratchpad]]
- And you can alias them: [[Reading List|what I'm reading]]

That's the whole toolbox. Back to [[Welcome to Scratchpad]].

notes.title: Project: Trailhead
notes.body:
# Project: Trailhead

A weekend side project: a trail-finder PWA that works offline. Tracking scope here so it doesn't live only in my head.

**Status:** in progress · **Target:** end of August

## Milestones

- [x] Nail down the idea (see [[Kickoff Meeting]])
- [x] Sketch the data model
- [ ] Offline tile caching
- [ ] Elevation profile chart
- [ ] Ship a TestFlight build

## Scope

| Feature | Priority | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Search trails by name | P0 | done |
| Offline maps | P0 | building |
| Save favorites | P1 | todo |
| Share a route | P2 | someday |

## Open questions

- Which tile provider allows offline caching on the free tier?
- Do I need accounts, or is local-only enough? (Leaning local-only — see how [[Welcome to Scratchpad]] does it.)

## Notes

Reading up on the fundamentals — tracking books in [[Reading List]].

notes.title: Kickoff Meeting
notes.body:
# Kickoff Meeting

**Date:** 2026-07-06 · **Project:** [[Project: Trailhead]]

## Attendees

| Name | Role |
| --- | --- |
| Me | Everything |
| Rubber duck | QA |

## Decisions

- Local-first, no backend for v1
- PWA over native to start
- Weekend cadence — ship something small every two weeks

## Action items

- [x] Register the domain
- [ ] Pick a map tile provider
- [ ] Draft the data model (moved to [[Project: Trailhead]])

## Parking lot

Ideas that came up but aren't for now live in [[Ideas Parking Lot]].

notes.title: Reading List
notes.body:
# Reading List

Books and articles queued up. Checking them off as I go.

## Currently reading

- [ ] *Offline-First Web Development* — halfway through

## Queue

- [ ] *The Design of Everyday Things*

- [x] *Deep Work*

Feeding ideas into [[Project: Trailhead]].

notes.title: Weeknight Pasta
notes.body:
# Weeknight Pasta

The 20-minute one. Serves 2.

## Ingredients

| Item | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| Spaghetti | 200 g |
| Garlic | 3 cloves |
| Olive oil | 3 tbsp |
| Chili flakes | to taste |
| Parmesan | a generous handful |
| Parsley | a small bunch |

## Steps

1. Boil the pasta in well-salted water. Reserve a cup of the water before draining.
2. Gently warm the sliced garlic and chili in the olive oil — don't let it brown.
3. Toss the drained pasta in the oil with a splash of pasta water until glossy.
4. Off the heat, fold in the parmesan and parsley.

> Reserve more pasta water than you think you need. It's the whole trick.

notes.title: Ideas Parking Lot
notes.body:
# Ideas Parking Lot

Half-formed things. No pressure to do any of them.

- A CLI that turns [[Reading List]] into a yearly stats page
- Elevation-aware routing for [[Project: Trailhead]]
- A tiny habit tracker built on daily notes
- [[Someday Project]] — no note exists yet, so this renders as a *phantom* link. Click it and Scratchpad offers to create the note.

Cross-pollinating from the [[Kickoff Meeting]] parking lot.

notes.title: Thu, Jul 16, 2026
notes.body:
## Tasks

- [x] Morning walk
- [x] Two hours on [[Project: Trailhead]]
- [ ] Reply to emails
- [ ] Grocery run — see [[Weeknight Pasta]]

## Notes

Good deep-work block this morning. Offline tile caching is trickier than expected; parked a note about it in [[Ideas Parking Lot]].

Felt scattered after lunch. Tomorrow: start with the hardest thing first.
